title: glechoma betonicifolia
intro: Glechoma betonicifolia, also known as Creeping Charlie, is a perennial herb belonging to the mint family (Lamiaceae) native to China and parts of Eastern Asia. It has been used in traditional medicine for its medicinal properties, including anti-inflammatory, antimicrobial, and antiviral effects. The plant is valued for its health benefits, which include reducing fever, relieving pain and inflammation, and aiding in the treatment of respiratory and gastrointestinal issues. Glechoma betonicifolia is often prepared into herbal teas, infusions, and tinctures for its medicinal use, and can also be consumed as a fresh herb or dried for later use.
